title: senecio peripotamus
side_effects: Senecio peripotamus can have side effects if used improperly, such as liver damage, jaundice, and abdominal pain due to its high content of pyrrolizidine alkaloids. Prolonged consumption of the plant can also lead to cardiovascular issues, including high blood pressure and irregular heartbeat. Additionally, improper use of Senecio peripotamus may cause gastrointestinal problems, including diarrhea, vomiting, and stomach ulcers. In severe cases, the plant's toxicity can even result in kidney failure and other life-threatening complications.
